text: This volume explores the persistent educational disparities facing children of Turkish immigrants across Austria, France, and Sweden. Despite being one of the most prominent immigrant populations in these nations, individuals of Turkish descent continue to encounter systemic discrimination and restricted pathways to success—barriers that are particularly visible within the school system. A central finding of this study is that institutional structures play a decisive role in shaping outcomes; specifically, countries that offer more supportive educational arrangements significantly enhance the prospects for upward economic mobility among the second generation.
